mechanism_narrative: Sustained declines in the federal funds rate reduce the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly boosting equity valuations over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 5.0%, labor market tightness accelerates wage growth and consumer spending, driving corporate revenue expectations higher and lifting equity prices over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 60% of instances where UNRATE drops below 5.0% over a 3-year test window.

mechanism_narrative: Strong manufacturing output combined with rising residential construction signals robust aggregate demand and business investment, which historically precedes positive equity returns as corporate earnings expand.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising INDPRO and HOUST fails to predict positive 60-day forward returns in more than 55% of out-of-sample instances over a 5-year period.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-inflation environment, aggressive monetary tightening compresses equity valuations by raising the risk-free rate and discounting future cash flows more heavily, depressing stock prices.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL exceeds 205 and FEDFUNDS rises month-over-month
failure_condition: Equity returns are not significantly negative when both CPIAUCSL exceeds 205 and FEDFUNDS increases, or the predictive power vanishes in a 3-year rolling walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic activity, which supports corporate earnings growth and drives positive equity returns over a one-week horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC for PAYEMS month-over-month changes against 5-day forward SPX returns remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When real GDP growth accelerates above 2.5% annualized, corporate profit margins expand due to pricing power and volume growth, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward equity returns are not significantly positive when GDPC1 growth exceeds 2.5%, or the signal hit rate drops below 50% in a 4-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of falling unemployment and stable federal funds rates indicates a healthy labor market without restrictive monetary policy, which historically supports equity risk premiums and positive forward returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal of declining UNRATE and flat FEDFUNDS fails to predict positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of instances over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During supply-constrained periods where industrial production stagnates while consumer financing remains elevated, inventory buildups and demand mismatches trigger earnings downgrades, depressing equity prices.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO flat for 3 months while RSAFS rises
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are not significantly negative when INDPRO stagnates and RSAFS expands, or the signal predictive IC drops below 0.01 over a 2-year test period.
